channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Documentary Highlights South Africa’s Unequal and Unsustainable Food System
item.description: A new documentary titled 'What Feeds Us' sheds light on the inequalities and environmental harm caused by South Africa’s industrialised agricultural system. The film, directed by Alex Hendricks, shows...

item.title: Supreme Court to Re-examine Escudero Campaign Donation Ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court has agreed to review a decision made by the Commission on Elections (Comelec) that cleared Senator Chiz Escudero of any wrongdoing regarding a P30-million campaign donation he receiv...

item.title: Fuel crisis in Australia severely impacts vanlifers and people living on the streets
item.description: Australia's ongoing fuel crisis is having a significant impact on vanlifers and vulnerable communities. For some, like Sharon, who planned a long road trip around the country in her customised van, so...

item.title: Police move to arrest Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja over contempt of Parliament
item.description: Police are actively seeking Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja following a directive from the Senate watchdog committee, which found him in contempt of Parliament after he ignored multiple summons. The C...

item.title: Legal dispute threatens to delay South Africa’s foot-and-mouth vaccination programme
item.description: The Red Meat Producers Organisation (RPO) has raised concerns that legal action by veterinary bodies could stall the rollout of the foot-and-mouth disease (FMD) vaccination campaign in South Africa, a...

item.title: U.S. Markets Edge Higher as Oil Tops $100 a Barrel, Energy Stocks Lead Gains
item.description: U.S. stock markets rose cautiously on Monday, reflecting both optimism and unease as energy prices continued to surge. The S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, and Nasdaq Composite all posted modest...

item.title: Delhi Government Plans Expansion of Piped Gas Network to 4 Lakh Additional Homes
item.description: The Delhi government is making a major push to expand the city’s piped natural gas (PNG) network by targeting about four lakh new connections in the coming financial year. This initiative is part of a...

item.title: Arnold Schwarzenegger honoured with honorary doctorate at Ulster University
item.description: Arnold Schwarzenegger visited Belfast to receive an honorary doctorate from Ulster University, recognising his contributions to public service, environmental advocacy, and the arts. The 78-year-old ac...

item.title: Lake Burley Griffin partially closed due to suspected blue-green algae outbreak
item.description: An outbreak of suspected blue-green algae has led to the closure of parts of Lake Burley Griffin to swimmers. On Monday, a bright green substance was seen across the Central Basin, with ducks observed...

item.title: Severe Weather Alert: PMD Forecasts Heavy Rains, Thunderstorms Across Pakistan from April 1
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a comprehensive weather advisory forecasting widespread rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds across most parts of the country from April 1 to 4...

item.title: B.C. police watchdog examines RCMP-involved shooting that left man seriously injured
item.description: The Independent Investigations Office (IIO) of British Columbia has launched an investigation after a man was seriously injured following a police-involved shooting involving the RCMP. The incident re...

item.title: Bob Dylan launches Patreon with eclectic AI lectures and literary content
item.description: Bob Dylan, the 84-year-old iconic singer-songwriter, has launched a Patreon account, a move that has left fans both intrigued and puzzled. Unlike other musicians who have opted for platforms like Subs...

item.title: Guinea's Liquidity Crisis and the Need for Restored Confidence
item.description: Guinea is currently grappling with a severe liquidity crisis that has rapidly escalated into a broader crisis of confidence within its financial system. The initial trigger was a disruption in early 2...

item.title: Canadian audit highlights irregularities in student visa approvals for Indian applicants
item.description: A recent audit conducted by the Auditor General of Canada has raised concerns over the management of student visas issued to Indian applicants. The report pointed out that while countries with a high ...

item.title: Ottawa taxi drivers continue legal fight against the city over compensation dispute
item.description: The City of Ottawa and local taxi drivers were back in court on March 30, 2026, as part of an ongoing lawsuit that dates back several years. The legal dispute stems from Ottawa’s decision to permit ri...

item.title: Israeli Strikes on Iranian Nuclear Facilities Highlight Global Complacency Risks
item.description: In a personal reflection, the author draws parallels between the sinking of the Titanic and current world events, particularly recent Israeli airstrikes on Iranian nuclear sites. While watching a docu...

item.title: Court rules paternity cannot currently be determined between identical twins
item.description: A woman who had sexual relations with identical twins within a span of four days has been informed that it is currently impossible to determine which twin is the father of her child. The case reached ...

item.title: Protest over alleged coronation of Nigerian traditional leader turns violent in KuGompo City
item.description: Police in the Eastern Cape have confirmed that twelve vehicles were damaged on Monday after a protest in KuGompo City over the reported coronation of a Nigerian ‘king’ turned violent. According to pol...

item.title: CAF Introduces Major Reforms to Improve Refereeing and Governance After AFCON 2025 Final Issues
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) has announced major reforms to its refereeing and governance systems following controversies in the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) final held in Morocco...

item.title: Egypt imposes early closing hours and fuel restrictions as oil prices soar due to Iran war
item.description: Egypt don introduce strict emergency measures to cut down energy use because of the global oil wahala wey follow the war around Iran and the blockade for Strait of Hormuz. From Saturday, all shops, re...
